gloss:
Away from resonance, two normalized qubit states become indistinguishable under repeated position-expectation measurement exactly when one is obtained from the other by a phase rotation, or by a conjugation-swap followed by a phase rotation. These physical symmetry orbits emerge from a single observable's dynamics even though no symmetry structure was supplied.

**Cites** `WMSpec.QubitSystem.pos_bisimilar_iff_wigner_orbit` — the corpus theorem this result restates and discharges.

**Original contribution.** The raw lemma characterizes an equivalence; the thesis-layer statement identifies that characterization with the classical symmetry-orbit structure recovered from dynamics alone, and slots it into the shared 'geometry is the answer, not the input' framing.

statement.pretty: ∀ (Q : WMSpec.QubitSystem) (t : ℝ), Real.sin (WMSpec.QubitSystem.ω Q * t) ≠ 0 → ∀ (ψ χ : ℂ × ℂ), WMSpec.QubitSystem.pop 0 ψ + WMSpec.QubitSystem.pop 1 ψ = 1 → WMSpec.QubitSystem.pop 0 χ + WMSpec.QubitSystem.pop 1 χ = 1 → ((TLT.NonIdentifiability.bisimilarity (fun x => WMSpec.QubitSystem.posExp Q) (TLT.NonIdentifiability.pureKernel (WMSpec.QubitSystem.U Q t))) ψ χ ↔ ∃ θ, χ = WMSpec.QubitSystem.phase θ ψ ∨ χ = WMSpec.QubitSystem.phase θ (WMSpec.QubitSystem.swapConj ψ))

title: Eidometry.Thesis.quantum_wigner_orbit
